meta.title: MergeCore React Rules
meta.description: Rules for modern React (function components, hooks): correctness, render safety, state ownership, accessibility, and performance.
meta.notes: Gate with react: true when the host supports project metadata. Rules marked react: true are React-specific.

scoring.normalisation: Per review unit (one component module), sum penalties for matched rules after optional quality_signal multiplier, then score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))).

interpretation.9_to_10: Idiomatic hooks, safe effects, stable keys, accessible markup.
interpretation.7_to_8: Mergeable; tighten effect deps, memoisation, or list keys.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky: stale closures, unbounded renders, or missing a11y.
interpretation.0_to_4: Block: XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML, broken state, or leaking effects.

rules.title: Hook called conditionally or inside a loop
rules.description: Hooks must run in the same order on every render. Calling useState/useEffect/useMemo conditionally or inside loops breaks React's internal bookkeeping.

prefer: Call hooks unconditionally at the top of the component

examples.after: const [x, setX] = useState(0); // branch on enabled inside handlers or render

rules.title: useEffect dependency array missing referenced values
rules.description: Omitting referenced props or state from the dependency array yields stale closures and subtle bugs. If omission is intentional, extract the value or use a ref with a clear comment.

heuristics: Effect body references props/state not present in the deps array

rules.title: Array index used as React key
rules.description: Using array index as a key causes incorrect reconciliation when items reorder, insert, or delete. Prefer a stable domain id.

rules.title: dangerouslySetInnerHTML with unsanitised input
rules.description: Injecting HTML from user or remote data without sanitisation enables XSS. Sanitise with a vetted library (e.g. DOMPurify) or render as text.

rules.title: setState based on previous state without updater function
rules.description: Reading state directly inside setState races with batched updates. Use the functional form when the next value depends on the current one.

rules.title: Async work in useEffect without cleanup or cancellation
rules.description: Unmounted components can still receive async results, causing state updates on dead components and memory leaks. Abort or gate on a cleanup flag.

examples.after: useEffect(() => { const ac = new AbortController(); fetch(url, { signal: ac.signal }).then(setData).catch(() => {}); return () => ac.abort(); }, [url])

rules.title: Inline object or array props on hot-rendering parents
rules.description: Fresh object/array literals on every render break memoised children. Memoise with useMemo or hoist to module scope where safe.

heuristics: Memoised child (React.memo) receiving a fresh literal every render

rules.title: Interactive element without accessible name or role
rules.description: Click handlers on divs/spans without role, tabIndex, or keyboard handlers exclude keyboard and screen-reader users. Prefer semantic buttons or links.

rules.title: Derived state stored in useState instead of computed
rules.description: Mirroring props into state produces drift. Compute derived values during render or with useMemo.

heuristics: useEffect only syncs a prop into local state

rules.title: Uncontrolled vs controlled input mismatch
rules.description: Switching an input between controlled (value) and uncontrolled (defaultValue) mid-lifecycle warns in dev and corrupts state. Pick one.

heuristics: value toggles between a string and undefined

rules.title: Strong pattern: memoised child with stable callbacks
rules.description: React.memo combined with useCallback for handlers signals deliberate render control for a hot path.

rules.title: Context used for local UI state
rules.description: Context causes every consumer to re-render on change. Prefer lifting state minimally or a dedicated store for widely shared, frequently changing values.

heuristics: Context value changes on every keystroke or tick
